title: Aegis Response Action
description: A concrete disruptive/response action the platform recommends off a detection or fusion event. This is the 'disrupt/respond' step — turning a detection into a timestamped, auditable action package aimed at a specific recipient (bank, telecom, MHA/I4C, investigating officer, or the citizen). Dispatch is SIMULATED for demonstration: no live government/bank/telecom integration is connected. The action never asserts guilt — it is decision-support with a full audit trail.

action_id.description: Stable id: '<action_type>:<primary_target>'. Stable so re-deriving does not wipe an officer's dispatch/acknowledge state.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the action was first proposed.

action_type.description: account_freeze = ask beneficiary bank/NPCI to freeze a mule account; telecom_block = ask DoT/operator to block a scam number; mha_alert = consolidated national alert to I4C/MHA; citizen_intercept = pre-transfer warning / transaction hold for a potential victim; review_queue = push an intelligence package to an investigating officer.

priority.description: critical = money actively moving or victim mid-transfer; high = active infrastructure to disable; medium = investigative follow-up.

status.description: proposed = system recommends it; dispatched = (simulated) sent to the recipient; acknowledged = recipient/officer confirmed receipt/action; dismissed = ruled out by an officer.

recipient.description: Who would action this in the real workflow (e.g. 'Beneficiary bank via NPCI', 'DoT / operator (CEIR)', 'I4C / MHA (NCRP 1930)', 'Investigating officer').

refs.description: Evidence event ids / ring ids that justify the action — the audit chain.

rationale.description: Plain-English reason this action is recommended.

target.description: What the action operates on. Fields present depend on action_type.

payload.description: The structured body that would be transmitted to the recipient (alert JSON, freeze request, etc.).

sla_minutes.description: Target time-to-action in minutes for this priority — used to show lead time against the fraud clock.

audit.description: Append-only status history. Every state change adds an entry — this is what makes the package auditable.

simulated.description: Always true in this build — dispatch is demonstrated, not wired to a live recipient.
